common.cancel_comment: Generic cancel button label used in confirmation dialogs (move subtasks, delete bucket, reset to defaults).

panel.title_comment: Plugin name shown as the sidebar panel tab title.
panel.title: GTD Tasks
panel.searchPlaceholder_comment: Placeholder text inside the search input at the top of the panel.
panel.clearSearch_comment: Tooltip on the × icon that clears the active search query.
panel.dismissAll_comment: Tooltip on the broom/sweep icon button in the panel header. Removes all completed tasks from view early (they would disappear automatically at midnight). Does NOT delete tasks.
panel.openSettings_comment: Tooltip on the gear icon button in the panel header. Opens the GTD Tasks settings tab specifically.
panel.searchStatus_comment: Shown below the search bar when a search is active. {{matched}} = number of visible tasks, {{total}} = total tasks across all buckets. The × at the end is a clickable clear button.
panel.noTasksFound_comment: Empty-state message shown in the panel body when no tasks exist in the configured scope (entire vault or specific folders/files). Not shown during search — that uses searchStatus with matched=0.
panel.noTasksFound: No tasks found. Adjust the task scope in settings.

moveConfirm._comment: Confirmation dialog shown when moving a parent task whose subtasks each have their own bucket assignment. The user chooses whether to move only the parent or all subtasks too.
moveConfirm.message_comment: {{count}} = number of subtasks that have an explicit bucket assigned.
moveConfirm.message: {{count}} subtasks have their own bucket assigned. Move them too?
moveConfirm.moveAll_comment: Button: move the parent task and all its subtasks to the new bucket.
moveConfirm.parentOnly_comment: Button: move only the parent task; leave subtasks in their current buckets.

contextMenu.moveTo_comment: Right-click context menu item on a task. {{name}} = the destination bucket's display name.
contextMenu.openFile_comment: Right-click context menu item on a task. Opens the source markdown file in the editor.

languageBanner.message_comment: Dismissable info banner shown once after the user changes their Obsidian interface language and restarts. 'Bucket names' are the GTD time-horizon categories (Today, This Week, etc.) — their display names may have been auto-renamed to match the new language.
languageBanner.message: Your Obsidian language has changed. Bucket names updated where possible — review in settings if needed.
languageBanner.openSettings_comment: Button in the language banner. Opens the GTD Tasks settings tab so the user can review bucket names. Different from panel.openSettings — same destination but triggered from the banner.
languageBanner.dismiss_comment: Button in the language banner. Closes the banner without opening settings.

task.staleTooltip_comment: Tooltip on tasks that are past their bucket's scheduled date window (e.g. a 'This Week' task that was never moved and is now overdue for that window).
task.autoPlacedTooltip_comment: Tooltip on tasks automatically assigned to a bucket based on their 📅 due date (written by the Tasks plugin). The user can click the task's bucket badge to confirm the placement and lock it in.
task.autoPlacedTooltip: Auto-placed from due date — click to confirm placement
task.subtaskTooltip_comment: Tooltip on the indentation badge shown on tasks that are nested under a parent task in the source markdown.
task.activeSubtasks_comment: Tooltip on the subtask count badge shown on parent tasks. i18next plural forms: _one for exactly 1, _other for all other counts. {{count}} is injected automatically by i18next.
task.dismiss_comment: Button label shown on completed tasks when 'show completed until midnight' is enabled. Removes the task from view immediately without waiting for midnight. Does NOT delete the task.
task.moveTo_comment: Label on quick-move buttons shown on each task row. {{name}} = the destination bucket's display name (e.g. 'Move to Today').
task.emptyTask_comment: Fallback text shown in the task row when the task's markdown text is completely blank.

commands.openPanel_comment: Label shown in Obsidian's command palette for the command that opens the GTD sidebar panel.

notices._comment: Toast notifications shown in Obsidian's notification area. All prefixed with 'GTD Tasks:' so users can identify their source. {{error}} is the raw error message from the failed operation.
notices.migrationComplete_comment: Shown after the user migrates tasks between storage modes (inline tag ↔ inline field). {{migrated}} = tasks successfully rewritten, {{failed}} = tasks skipped due to errors.
notices.migrationComplete: GTD Tasks: Migration complete. {{migrated}} tasks updated, {{failed}} skipped.

settings.heading_comment: Top-level section heading shown in the Obsidian settings panel above all plugin settings.
settings.heading: Getting Things Done

banner.message_comment: Personal thank-you note from the author, shown at the top of the settings tab. Stands alone as a complete sentence — no link is embedded in it.
banner.message: Thanks for using GTD Tasks! I built it to manage my own work every day, and I hope it makes yours a little easier too. I'd genuinely appreciate hearing what's working, what's not, or what you wish it did.
banner.buttonText_comment: Label on the call-to-action button next to the message. Opens the GitHub issues page in the default browser.
banner.signature_comment: Sign-off shown under the message, on its own line. A name, not translated per-locale.

settings.emoji_comment: Label for the emoji input field shown in each bucket's configuration row.
settings.emojiButtonTooltip_comment: Tooltip on the emoji character button next to the emoji input. Clicking it opens Obsidian's native emoji picker.

annotationStyle.name_comment: Setting name for the storage mode selector — controls how bucket assignments are written into markdown.
annotationStyle.name: Annotation style
annotationStyle.inlineTag_comment: Dropdown option label. In this mode the plugin appends a hashtag like #gtd/today to the task line.
annotationStyle.inlineField_comment: Dropdown option label. In this mode the plugin appends a Dataview-compatible field like [gtd:: today] to the task line.
annotationStyle.descHeader_comment: Introductory sentence before the two bullet-point descriptions below it.
annotationStyle.descHeader: Controls how the plugin stores which bucket each task belongs to:
annotationStyle.descInlineTag_comment: Bullet-point description for the inline-tag mode. The example uses the user's configured prefix (default: gtd).
annotationStyle.descInlineTag: Inline tag — appends #prefix/bucket-id to the task line (e.g. #gtd/today)
annotationStyle.descInlineField_comment: Bullet-point description for the inline-field mode. Dataview is a popular Obsidian plugin for querying notes.
annotationStyle.descInlineField: Inline field — appends [prefix:: bucket-id] to the task line (Dataview-compatible)

migrate._comment: The migrate button rewrites all existing bucket annotations from one storage mode to the other (e.g. all #gtd/today tags → [gtd:: today] fields). Pluralized on {{count}} = number of tasks that need rewriting.
migrate.none_comment: Shown on the migrate button when all tasks are already in the current storage mode — nothing to rewrite.
migrate.inProgress_comment: Shown on the migrate button while the migration is running.

tagFieldName.name_comment: Setting name for the tag/field prefix input (e.g. the 'gtd' part of #gtd/today).
tagFieldName.name: Tag / field name
tagFieldName.description: Used as the tag prefix in Inline tag mode (#<name>/bucket-id) and as the field key in Inline field mode ([<name>:: bucket-id]).
tagFieldName.placeholder_comment: Placeholder text inside the prefix input showing the default value.

tasksPlugin._comment: Toggle to enable reading metadata written by the 'Tasks' community plugin (emoji-based due/completion dates). {{pluginName}} is substituted at runtime with the plugin's display name ('Tasks').
tasksPlugin.name: Read {{pluginName}} plugin metadata
tasksPlugin.description: Read 📅 due dates and ✅ completion dates written by the {{pluginName}} plugin.

filesToScan.name_comment: Setting name for the scope selector — determines which files are scanned for tasks.
filesToScan.name: Files to scan
filesToScan.description: Which files the plugin searches for tasks.
filesToScan.entireVault_comment: Dropdown option: scan every markdown file in the vault.
filesToScan.specificFolders_comment: Dropdown option: scan only the folders listed below.
filesToScan.specificFiles_comment: Dropdown option: scan only the individual files listed below.
filesToScan.addFolder_comment: Button label to add a folder path to the scan list. The leading '+' is part of the label.
filesToScan.addFile_comment: Button label to add a file path to the scan list. The leading '+' is part of the label.
filesToScan.folderPlaceholder_comment: Placeholder inside each folder path input showing an example value.
filesToScan.filePlaceholder_comment: Placeholder inside each file path input showing an example value.
filesToScan.noFoldersSelected_comment: Shown above the add-folder button when the folder list is empty.
filesToScan.noFoldersSelected: No folders selected. The plugin won't scan anything for tasks.
filesToScan.noFilesSelected_comment: Shown above the add-file button when the file list is empty.
filesToScan.noFilesSelected: No files selected. The plugin won't scan anything for tasks.

behaviour.heading_comment: Section heading for the Behaviour group of settings (completed-task visibility, overdue marking, compact view, celebration animations). Shown above the group and used as its search label in Obsidian 1.13.0+.
behaviour.heading: Behaviour

showCompleted.name: Show completed tasks until midnight
showCompleted.description_comment: The 'strikethrough' refers to the visual style of completed tasks in the panel.
showCompleted.description: Completed tasks remain visible as strikethrough in the panel until midnight, then disappear on next load.

markOverdue.name_comment: The '!' is the literal character displayed as an overdue indicator badge on the task row.
markOverdue.name: Mark overdue tasks with !
markOverdue.description: Tasks that are past their bucket's scheduled window are marked with an overdue indicator.

compactView.name: Compact view
compactView.description: Reduce padding on bucket headers and task rows to fit more tasks on screen.

celebration.name: Celebration animations
celebration.description_comment: Dropdown description. Two independent animation types can play on task completion: confetti (particles raining down) and a celebration image (an animal illustration bouncing in).
celebration.description: Choose which animations play when you complete a task.
celebration.confettiOnly_comment: Dropdown option: play only the confetti particle effect.
celebration.celebrationOnly_comment: Dropdown option: play only the animal illustration bounce animation.
celebration.both_comment: Dropdown option: play both animations simultaneously.

buckets.heading_comment: Section heading above the bucket list in settings.
buckets.heading: Buckets
buckets.moveUp_comment: Tooltip on the ↑ reorder button for a bucket row in the settings list.
buckets.moveDown_comment: Tooltip on the ↓ reorder button for a bucket row in the settings list.
buckets.addBucket_comment: Button at the bottom of the bucket list that creates a new empty bucket.
buckets.newBucketName_comment: Default display name given to a newly created bucket before the user renames it.
buckets.resetToDefaults_comment: Button that discards all bucket configuration and restores the five default GTD buckets.
buckets.resetConfirm_comment: Confirmation prompt shown in a dialog before resetting. Warns that the current configuration will be lost.
buckets.resetConfirm: Reset all buckets to defaults? This will discard your current bucket configuration.
buckets.resetButton_comment: Confirm button inside the reset confirmation dialog.
buckets.systemBucket_comment: Badge shown next to the 'To Review' bucket in the settings list. It is a built-in system bucket and cannot be deleted.

showInStatusBar.name_comment: Section heading for the status bar visibility group in settings.
showInStatusBar.name: Show in status bar
showInStatusBar.description: Display the {{bucketName}} count in the status bar.

field.name_comment: Label for the bucket display name text input.
field.name: Name
field.id_comment: Label for the bucket ID input. 'Slug' is a URL-safe identifier — lowercase, no spaces. This value is what gets written into markdown (e.g. the 'today' in #gtd/today).
field.idDescription_comment: Helper text below the ID input.
field.idDescription: Internal identifier used in tags/fields. No spaces.
field.idDuplicate_comment: Inline validation error when the user types an ID already in use. {{id}} = the duplicate value entered, {{existing}} = display name of the bucket that already owns that ID.
field.showInStatusBar_comment: Toggle label inside an individual bucket's config row (as opposed to settings.buckets.showInStatusBar.name which is a section heading).
field.showInStatusBarDescription: Display this bucket's task count in the status bar.
field.delete_comment: Label on the inline delete icon/button on each bucket row.
field.deleteTooltip_comment: Tooltip on the delete icon button.
field.deleteConfirm_comment: Confirmation prompt in a dialog before deleting a bucket. {{name}} = the bucket's display name.
field.deleteConfirm: Delete bucket "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
field.deleteButton_comment: Confirm button inside the delete confirmation dialog (as opposed to field.delete which is the inline trigger).

dateRule._comment: Date range rules auto-assign tasks to a bucket based on their 📅 due date. The dropdown option labels below describe the rule type. Calendar weeks run Monday–Sunday.
dateRule.name: Date range rule
dateRule.description: Auto-assign tasks based on their 📅 due date. If multiple buckets have overlapping rules, the first matching bucket (top to bottom) wins.
dateRule.none_comment: Dropdown option: no automatic date-based assignment; tasks are only placed by manual tag/field.
dateRule.thisWeek: This week (tomorrow – end of current Sunday)
dateRule.thisMonth: This month (1 day out – end of current month)
dateRule.nextMonth: Next month (1st – last day of next month)
dateRule.withinDays_comment: Dropdown option: tasks due within a user-configured number of days from today.
dateRule.withinRange_comment: Dropdown option: tasks due within a user-configured day range (from day X to day Y from today).
dateRule.beyondDays_comment: Dropdown option: catch-all rule for tasks due further out than a user-configured number of days.

days.name: Days
days.description: Tasks due within this many days from today are assigned here.

fromDay.name: From day
fromDay.description: Start of the window: number of days from today (e.g. 1 = tomorrow, 7 = one week from today).

toDay.name: To day
toDay.description: End of the window: number of days from today (e.g. 14 = two weeks from today).

beyondThreshold.name: Days threshold
beyondThreshold.description: Tasks due more than this many days from today are assigned here.

quickMove._comment: Each task row can show up to two quick-move buttons for common destinations. These settings let the user configure which buckets those buttons target.
quickMove.button1Name_comment: Setting name for the primary (first) quick-move button target.
quickMove.button2Name_comment: Setting name for the optional secondary quick-move button target.
quickMove.button1Description: Primary quick-move target shown on each task row.
quickMove.none_comment: Dropdown option meaning no bucket is assigned to this quick-move button slot (button hidden).

buckets._comment: Default display names for the five built-in GTD buckets. Keys (today, this-week, etc.) are the fixed internal IDs written into the vault — they never change regardless of language. Only the 'name' values are translatable.

someday.name_comment: 'Someday / Maybe' is a standard GTD (Getting Things Done) methodology term for tasks with no fixed time horizon.
someday.name: Someday / Maybe

buckets.toReview_comment: Display name of the built-in system bucket that collects tasks with no bucket assignment. Always shown first in the panel. Cannot be renamed or deleted by the user.
